EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Clearing skies and a cold air mass made for freezing temperatures along the Oregon Coast on this date.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Reported low temperatures ranged from 29 to 37 degrees.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong and cold winter storm brought heavy snow and strong winds to southern Oregon...mostly from the Cascades east and in the Kalmiopsis Wilderness.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Several locations in this zone recorded heavy precipitation during this interval, including 3.30 inches at Fourmile Lake, 2.10 inches at Bigelow Camp, 2.00 inches at Billie Creek, 2.60 inches at Cold Springs, and 2.23 inches at Zim.  Additionally, Crater Lake National Park in adjacent zone ORZ027 reported 18.0 inches of snow in 24 hours.  Based on these reports, it is assumed that heavy snow fell in portions of zone ORZ028.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong and cold winter storm brought heavy snow and strong winds to southern Oregon...mostly from the Cascades east and in the Kalmiopsis Wilderness.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A spotter 2NNW Klamath Falls reported 6.0 inches of heavy wet snow at 18/1500 PST.  Based on other spotter reports nearby, it is assumed that this snow fell in the previous nine hours.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Clearing skies and a cold air mass made for cold low temperatures along parts of the Oregon coast this morning.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Reported low temperatures ranged from 30 to 34 degrees.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: The third in a series of strong storms brought high winds to the Southern Oregon coast as well as portions of South Central Oregon.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The ODOT sensor at Port Orford recorded several gusts in the 58-60 mph range during this interval.  The peak gust of 60 mph was recorded at 12/0918 PST.

CZ_NAME: NORTHERN & EASTERN KLAMATH COUNTY & WESTERN LAKE COUNTY
EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Several incoming fronts induced a nearly continuous high wind event over south central Oregon east of the Cascades.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Calimus RAWS recorded gusts exceeding 57 mph continuously during this interval.  The peak gust was 68 mph, recorded at 31/0700 PST.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Several incoming fronts induced a nearly continuous high wind event over south central Oregon east of the Cascades.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Summer Lake RAWS recorded gusts exceeding 57 mph frequently from 29/0438 until 30/0438 PST, then occasionally during the rest of the warning period.  The peak gust of 66 mph occurred at 31/2138 PST.  The Summit RAWS recorded gusts to 58 mph at 29/0403 PST and 31/0903 PST and a gust to 61 mph at 31/2203 PST.  A spotter 9SSE Summer Lake recorded a gust to 65 mph at 29/1653 PST.  He also reported a gust to 72 mph at 29/1530 PST.  The Lakeview Interagency Fire Center reported wind gusts exceeding 50 mph and the winds took down a power pole, causing a 20 acre wildfire along Highway 140 near Adel on the morning of the 31st.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An incoming front brought strong winds to South Central Oregon east of the Cascades.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A member of the public reported a barn blown down at Adel, with debris scattered 100 yards onto a nearby roadway.

CZ_NAME: NORTHERN & EASTERN KLAMATH COUNTY & WESTERN LAKE COUNTY
EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An incoming front brought strong winds to South Central Oregon east of the Cascades.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A member of the public reported a measured wind gust to 63 mph at Lorella at 24/2217.  The Calimus RAWS recorded a gust to 60 mph at 24/2200 PST.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: The third in a series of strong storms brought high winds to the Southern Oregon coast as well as portions of South Central Oregon.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Flynn Prairie RAWS recorded a gust to 58 mph at 22/0613 PST and a gust to 59 mph at 22/1013 PST.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A warm front carrying copious moisture from the Pacific moved into Southest Oregon spreading heavy snow over parts of Northern Harney County impacting the U.S. Highway 20 corridor.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Trained observers and spotters reported five inches of new snow around Burns and eight inches at Drewsey.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An incoming cold front brought high winds to the coast and areas east of the Cascades.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A sensor near Port Orford reported winds gusting in excess of 58 mph from 28/1436 through 28/1630 PST.  The peak gust was 65 mph recorded at 28/1600 PST.

CZ_NAME: NORTHERN & EASTERN KLAMATH COUNTY & WESTERN LAKE COUNTY
EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An incoming cold front brought high winds to the coast and areas east of the Cascades.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Calimus RAWS recorded a wind gust to 63 mph at 28/2100 PST.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An incoming cold front brought high winds to the coast and areas east of the Cascades.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Summer Lake RAWS recorded a wind gust to 69 mph at 28/1838 PST.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Strong winds aloft surfaced in the Summer Lake area.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Summer Lake RAWS recorded gusts greater than 57 mph numerous times during this interval.  Peak gusts to 68 mph were recorded at 24/1138, 24/1238, and 24/1838 PST.  A spotter near Summer Lake reported a gust to 58 mph at 24/1421 PST.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Very heavy and sustained precipitation led to a widespread flooding event over several counties in Southwest Oregon.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Mountain Man RV Park about halfway between O'Brien and Cave Junction had to be partially evacuated due to flooding.  A storm total of 12.70 inches of rain was reported by a spotter 2WSW O'Brien.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Sustained heavy rain led to flooding on a number of rivers and creeks in Southwest Oregon.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Elk Creek gage near Drain crested at 15.55 feet at 19/1630 PST, below the flood stage of 16.00 feet.  However, flooding was reported a few miles downstream from the gage, with damage to yards and fences.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Heavy precipitation led to a major flooding event on the South Fork of the Coquille River at Myrtle Point.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The South Fork of the Coquille River at Myrtle Point reached the flood stage of 33.00 feet at 19/1315 PST. It exceeded the moderate flood stage of 35.00 feet at 19/1530 PST, then exceeded the major flood stage of 37.00 feet at 19/2100 PST.  It crested at 37.04 feet at 19/2130 PST, then receded below major flood stage at 19/2145 PST. It receded below moderate flood stage at 20/0630 PST, then receded below flood stage at 20/1315 PST.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Two incoming cold fronts brought periods of high winds to the southern Oregon coast.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A sensor at Port Orford reported numerous gust to 58 and 59 mph during this interval.  The peak gust was 59 mph, recorded at 29/1142, 29/1148, and 29/1224 PST.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Yet another front brought yet another round of high winds to the southern Oregon coast.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The sensor at Port Orford recorded numerous gusts exceeding 57 mph during this interval, mostly between 31/1230 and 31/1448 PST.  The peak gust was 64 mph, recorded at 31/1418 PST.  The Long Prairie RAWS recorded a lone gust to 59 mph at 31/2113 PST.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Sustained heavy precipitation brought flooding to main stem rivers and other areas of southwest Oregon.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The mainstem Coquille River and the South and North Forks of the Coquille River all reached levels that are associated with flooding on feeder streams as well as the Coos and Millacoma rivers.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Sustained heavy precipitation brought flooding to main stem rivers and other areas of southwest Oregon.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Elks and Sixes rivers showed impacts from flooding.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Sustained heavy precipitation brought flooding to main stem rivers and other areas of southwest Oregon.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Deer Creek at Roseburg reached flood stage in this event and this stream is considered to be representative of other small streams in the area.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain brought another round of areal flooding to southwest Oregon.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Chetco, Elk, and Sixes rivers were all impacted by flooding.  It is likely that other streams in the area such as the Pistol and Winchuck were similarly impacted.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain brought another round of areal flooding to southwest Oregon.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Flooding was reported on the west fork of the Illinois river.  Flooding was reported at Mountain Man RV Park 2N Cave Junction.  Slate Creek near Selma was reported out of its banks.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Strong winds combined with heavy swell led to high surf along the Southern Oregon coast.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Umpqua Offshore Buoy 46229 recorded swell that would translate into surf meeting warning criteria during this interval.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Strong winds combined with heavy swell led to high surf along the Southern Oregon coast.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Umpqua Offshore Buoy 46229 recorded swell that would translate into surf meeting warning criteria during this interval.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A storm system brought snow to lower elevations than usual.  A Winter Weather Advisory was issued for this system, but one report verified a Heavy Snow Warning.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A spotter 10NW Tiller reported 5.1 inches of snow in 11 hours ending at 25/1000 PST.

CZ_NAME: NORTHERN & EASTERN KLAMATH COUNTY & WESTERN LAKE COUNTY

EVENT_NARRATIVE: The cooperative observer at Chemult reported 16 inches of snow in 24 hours ending at 29/1700 PST.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A major winter storm brought heavy snow to unusually low elevations in southwest Oregon.  The higher elevations also got heavy snow.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A spotter 2E Glendale reported 5 inches of snow overnight ending at 29/0730 PST.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A major winter storm brought heavy snow to unusually low elevations in southwest Oregon.  The higher elevations also got heavy snow.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A major winter storm brought heavy snow to unusually low elevations in southwest Oregon.  The higher elevations also got heavy snow.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The cooperative observer at Toketee Falls reported 7.0 inches of snow in 24 hours ending at 29/1900 PST.

CZ_NAME: EAST SLOPES OF THE OREGON CASCADES
EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Heavy snowfall which began on February 28 finally ended early March 1.  Snowfall amounts measured in inches included Sunriver (13) and 5 miles northeast of Sisters (6).
Icy roads caused numerous accidents and the closure of I-84 between LaGrande and Pendleton.
A strong Pacific cold front brought strong southerly winds to the north Oregon Coast.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Strong winds were reported at Clatsop Spit with sustained wind of 41 kt and gusts to 50 kt.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: With a cold air mass in place over the Pacific Northwest, two strong and very moist Pacific weather systems brought widespread heavy rains to the Willamette Valley flooding many urban and small streams.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Widespread low-land flooding occurred in Marion County, resulting in considerable flood damage to homes in southeast Salem and Turner. Residents of 300 homes in Turner had to be evacuated, including 90 residents of the Turner Retirement Home.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: With a cold air mass in place over the Pacific Northwest, two strong and very moist Pacific weather systems brought widespread heavy rains to the Willamette Valley flooding many urban and small streams.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Widespread low-land flooding occurred in Linn County, resulting in considerable flood damage to homes in Scio. 870 residents of Scio had to be evacuated.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong low pressure system moved over Western Oregon from the southwest Tuesday night and Wednesday bringing very strong winds to the North and Central Oregon Coast and Coast Range Wednesday morning.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Strong winds were reported at the Tidewater RAWS site with a gust to 61 kt. A trained spotter in Blodgett reported the wind snapping large tree limbs.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: The stormy pattern continued as another cold front brought high winds to portions of southern Oregon.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Squaw Peak RAWS recorded numerous gusts exceeding 74 mph during this interval.  The peak gust was 99 mph at 20/0639 and 20/0739 PST.  The ZIM RAWS also recorded several high wind gusts.  The peak gust was 81 mph recorded at 20/0639 PST.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: The stormy pattern continued as another cold front brought high winds to portions of southern Oregon.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Summer Lake RAWS recorded several gusts exceeding 57 mph during this interval.  The peak gust was 61 mph at 20/0038 PST.  The Summit RAWS recorded a peak gust if 58 mph at 20/1103 PST. A sensor in Christmas Valley recorded a gust to 58 mph at 20/0600 PST.  A spotter at Valley Falls recorded a gust to 62 mph at around 20/0109 PST and a gust to 74 mph around 20/1000 PST.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Persistent heavy rains led to flooding on the Coquille River.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Coquille River remained above flood state during this interval.  The river crested at 22.89 feet at 23/2245 PST.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong low pressure system moved over Western Oregon from the southwest Tuesday night and Wednesday bringing very strong winds to the North and Central Oregon Coast and Coast Range Wednesday morning.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Strong winds were reported at Pacific City with sustained wind of 50 kt and gusts to 68 kt. Strong winds were also reported at Cape Meares with sustained wind of 39 kt and gusts to 66 kt, and at Garibaldi NOS with gusts to 60 kt. Power lines were reported to be down in Tillamook by a trained spotter when wind gusted to 56 kt. Strong wind gusts were also reported at Tillamook BPA with 57 kt, Tierra Del Mar CWOP with 54 kt and at the Tillamook AWOS site with 50 kt.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: Strong winds were reported at Timberline Lodge with sustained wind of 65 kt and gusts to 96 kt. This sensor is located at 7000 feet, however it is reasonable to assume that winds of at least 65 kt were blowing at 6000 feet.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An unusually cold air mass combined with clearing skies to bring freezing temperatures to portions of the southern Oregon coast.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Reported low temperatures ranged from 29 to 39 degrees.

The ODOT sensor at Gold Beach recorded a gust to 60 mph at 12/1904 PST and a gust to 62 mph at 12/2003 PST. A spotter SSW Gold Beach reported a gust to 80 mph overnight between the 12th and 13th.  A spotter 1ENE Brookings reported a gust to 60 mph at 12/1530 PST.

The Summit RAWS recorded numerous gusts in excess of 57 mph between 12/1603 and 13/0103 PST.  The peak gust was 70 mph, recorded at 12/2203 PST.The Coffee Pot Flat RAWS recorded a gust to 64 mph at 12/2244 PST and a gust to 63 mph at 13/0444 PST.  The Rock Creek RAWS recorded a gust to 62 mph at 13/0127 PST and a gust to 61 mph at 13/0327 PST. A spotter at Silver Lake recorded a gust to 60 mph at 12/1530 PST.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong Pacific weather system brought strong south winds to the North and Central Oregon Coast.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Strong winds were reported at Yachats with a gust to 65 kt. Strong winds were also reported at the Newport AWOS site with sustained winds of 34 kt and gusts to 51 kt, and at the Yaquina Bridge with a gust to 50 kt. Strong gusts were also reported at Gleneden Beach with a gust to 41 kt and at the Florence/Siuslaw Jetty with a gust to 40 kt.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Another in a series of strong Pacific frontal systems brought strong winds to the North and Central Oregon Coast.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Strong winds were reported at the Yaquina Bridge with sustained winds of 44 kt and gusts to 58 kt.  Strong winds were also reported at the Newport C-Man site with sustained winds of 43 kt and gusts to 55 kt. Strong gusts were reported at Lincoln City with a gust to 56 kt, at Cape Foulweather with a gust to 53 kt, and at the Florence/Siuslaw Jetty with a gust to 52 kt.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: The last in the series of strong Pacific frontal systems to affect the Pacific Northwest in January moved through the area Tuesday bringing strong winds to the North and Central Oregon Coast again.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Strong winds were reported by a trained spotter in Tillamook with gusts between 52 and 56 kt. Strong wind gusts were reported at Astoria with a gust to 53 kt and at Tierra Del Mar with a gust to 52 kt. Clatsop Spit reported sustained winds of 39 kt.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: The last in the series of strong Pacific frontal systems to affect the Pacific Northwest in January moved through the area Tuesday bringing strong winds to the North and Central Oregon Coast again.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Strong winds were reported at Cape Foulweather with a gust to 58 kt. Strong winds were also reported at Yaquina Bridge with sustained winds of 41 kt with gusts to 55 kt and the Florence/Siuslaw Jetty with sustained winds of 43 kt. Strong wind gusts were reported at Lincoln City with a gust to 56 kt and at Yachats with a gust to 54 kt.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: With a cold air mass in place over the Pacific Northwest, two strong and very moist Pacific weather systems brought snow to the area with heavy snow in the mountains and snow levels down to the valley floor.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A trained spotter in Parkdale measured 23 inches of new snow...with 13 of those inches falling in just 9 hours.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Dense freezing fog and icy roads with a dusting of snow caused a 12 vehicle crash with minor injuries on I-84 near Deadman's Pass.  Visibility was reduced to 100 feet or less in spots.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: A spotter NE Lakeside reported 5.0 inches of snow between 12/2000 and 12/2325 PST.  A spotter 3SSE Coos Bay reported 6.0 inches of snow through 13/0000 PST with a power failure.  A spotter 2SE Coos Bay reported 4.0 inches of snow between 12/2200 and 13/0400 PST.  A spotter 5NNE North Bend reported 5.0 to 6.0 inches of snow overnight.  A spotter 1NNW Lakeside reported 5.5 inches of snow overnight.  A member of the public reported 4.0 inches of snow in Coos Bay overnight with a power failure.  A spotter 7NNE Powers reported 3.5 inches of snow overnight.  A CoCoRAHS observer 1.7WNW Coos Bay reported 4.5 inches of snow.  Another CoCoRAHS observer 0.6WNW of Coos Bay reported 4.0 inches of snow.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: With a cold air mass in place over the Pacific Northwest, two strong and very moist Pacific weather systems brought snow to the area with heavy snow in the mountains and snow levels down to the valley floor.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Sunset Summit measured 24 inches of new snow. Heavy snowfall was also measured at Saddle Mountain with 23 inches of new snow, at the Wilson River Summit with 22 inches of new snow and at Seine Creek with 13 inches of new snow.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Along with the storms, heavy swell moved into the coastal waters, which created high surf conditions along the coast.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Umpqua Offshore Buoy recorded heavy swell during this interval with a period that was long enough to create high surf warning conditions along the coast.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Vigorous showers moved across the Central Oregon Coast and produced strong wind gusts.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Strong winds were reported at the Yaquina Bay Bridge in Newport with gusts to 58 kts. Wind gusts of 62 kts were also reported at Yachats.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Yet another incoming cold front brought high winds to portions of southern Oregon.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Squaw Peak RAWS recorded a gust to 76 mph at 04/0139 PST and a gust to 82 mph at 04/1439 PST.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Yet another incoming cold front brought high winds to portions of southern Oregon.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Summer Lake RAWS recorded several gusts exceeding 57 mph during this interval.  The peak gust was 61 mph recorded at 04/0638 PST. A spotter SSW Silver Lake reported south winds 40 mph gusting to 65 to 70 mph at 04/1144 PST.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: After a lull in storm activity, a strong cold front brought high winds back to portions of southern Oregon.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Port Orford platform recorded numerous gusts exceeding 57 mph during this interval.  The peak gust was 64 mph at 16/1812 PST.  The Long Prairie RAWS recorded many gusts exceeding 57 mph during this interval.  The peak gust was 70 mph at 03/2113 PST.  The North Bend airport recorded a gust to 60 mph at 16/1935 PST.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: After a lull in storm activity, a strong cold front brought high winds back to portions of southern Oregon.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Flynn Prairie RAWS recorded gusts to 62 mph at 16/1713 PST and 63 mph at 16/1813 PST.  The Gold Beach ASOS reported a gust to 62 mph at 16/1839 PST.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A long period of heavy rain led to flooding on the Coquille River at Coquille.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Coquille River at Coquille exceeded minor flood stage (21.0 feet) at 20/1800 PST, exceeded moderate flood stage (23.0 feet) at 21/0330 PST, fell below 23.0 feet at around 22/0415 PST, and fell below 21.0 feet at 22/2100 PST.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A long period of heavy rain led to flooding on the South Fork of the Coquille River at Myrtle Point.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The South Fork of the Coquille River at Myrtle Point exceeded minor flood stage (33.0 feet) at 20/1400 PST, exceeded moderate flood stage (35.0 feet) at 20/1645 PST, fell below 35.0 feet at 20/2000 PST, and fell below 33.0 feet at 21/0345 PST.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Another strong cold front brought high winds to parts of southern Oregon.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The NOS/NWLON platform at Port Orford recorded a gust to 60 mph at 29/0254 PST.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Another strong cold front brought high winds to parts of southern Oregon.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Flynn Prairie RAWS recorded a number of gusts exceeding 57 mph during this interval.  The peak gusts of 66 mph was recorded at 29/0013 PST.  Gold Beach recorded a gust to 59 mph at 29/0259 PST.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Another strong cold front brought high winds to parts of southern Oregon.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Squaw Peak RAWS recorded numerous gusts exceeding 75 mph during this interval.  The peak gust of 105 mph was recorded at 29/0439 PST.

CZ_NAME: NORTHERN & EASTERN KLAMATH COUNTY & WESTERN LAKE COUNTY
EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Another strong cold front brought high winds to parts of southern Oregon.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Another front brought high winds to the coast and ridges of southern Oregon, but only a few reports meeting warning criteria were received.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An unusually cold air mass led to late season freezing temperatures over some of the west side valleys of Southwest Oregon.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Reported low temperatures ranged from 23 to 27 degrees.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Cold air behind a Pacific cold front brought widespread showers to western Oregon and significant snowfall to the North Oregon Cascades and the Cascades in Lane County.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Red Hill SNOTEL site measured 20 inches of new snow. Other reports of heavy snow were 18 inches  at the Mud Ridge SNOTEL site and 16 inches at both the Blazed Alder SNOTEL site and the Little Meadows SNOTEL site.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Cold air behind a Pacific cold front brought widespread showers to western Oregon and significant snowfall to the North Oregon Cascades and the Cascades in Lane County.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The McKenzie SNOTEL site measured 25 inches of new snow. Another report of heavy snow was 21 inches at the Roaring River SNOTEL site.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: After a lull in storm activity, a strong cold front brought high winds back to portions of southern Oregon.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: After a lull in storm activity, a strong cold front brought high winds back to portions of southern Oregon.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Several RAWS recorded gusts exceeding 57 mph during this interval.  The peak gust at Rock Creek RAWS was 64 mph at 17/0127 PST.  The peak gust at Summer Lake RAWS was 62 mph recorded at 16/1538 PST.  He peak gust at Summit RAWS was 65 mph at 17/0203 PST.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Another cold front with strong southwest winds aloft brought high winds to the ridges and South Central Oregon.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Clear skies under offshore flow allowed temperatures in sheltered areas to drop below freezing.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Reported low temperatures ranged from 25 to 34 degrees.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: Reported low temperatures ranged from 29 to 33 degrees.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Heavy rains brought more main stem river flooding to southwest Oregon.  The Coquillle River flooded in two locations and Deer Creek flooded at Roseburg.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Heavy rains brought more main stem river flooding to southwest Oregon.  The Coquillle River flooded in two locations and Deer Creek flooded at Roseburg.

It crested at 38.57 feet at 22/0230 PST.  It fell below major flood stage at 22/0915 PST, fell below moderate flood stage at 22/1500 PST, then fell below flood stage at 22/2200 PST.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Heavy rains brought more main stem river flooding to southwest Oregon.  The Coquillle River flooded in two locations and Deer Creek flooded at Roseburg.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Deer Creek at Roseburg exceeded flood stage at 21/1400 PST, crested at 11.35 feet at 22/0100 PST, then fell below flood stage at 22/0430 PST.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An incoming front combined with strong upper level flow led to strong winds surfacing east of the Cascades.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Summer Lake RAWS recorded several gusts exceeded 57 mph during this interval.  The strongest gust was 65 mph, recorded at 20/1338 PST.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: This winter weather episode continues into March. An unstable air mass following a Pacific cold front brought widespread snow showers to the North Oregon Cascades and foothills, the Cascades in Lane County and the North Oregon Coastal Range.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Santiam Pass measured 36 inches of new snow. Heavy snowfall was also measured at Tombstone Pass with 34 inches of new snow, Santiam Junction with 33 inches of new snow, Timberline with 32 inches of new snow, and the Daly Lake SNOTEL site with 28 inches of new snow. Marion Forks and the Little Meadows SNOTEL site both measured 29 inches of new snow and both the Red Hill SNOTEL and Jump Off Joe SNOTEL sites measured 26 inches of new snow. 24 inches of new snow fell at the North Fork SNOTEL site and 21 inches of new snow fell at the Mt. Hood Test Site, Blue Box Pass and Government Camp. The Blazed Alder SNOTEL and the Peavine Ridge SNOTEL sites measured 20 inches of new snow, the Mud Ridge SNOTEL site measured 19 inches of new snow and Greenpoint measured 18 inches of new snow.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: The RAWS platform at Flynn Prairie recorded several gusts exceeding 57 mph during this interval.  The peak gust was 70 mph at 30/2013 PST. The Gold Beach observing platform recorded a gust to 60 mph at 30/2019 PST and a gust to 58 mph at 30/2239 PST.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Cold air following a very strong Pacific frontal system brought widespread low elevation snow to Western Oregon Monday night/Tuesday morning. Two more strong weather systems in this series brought heavy rains Wednesday and Thursday. The widespread heavy rain combined with melting snow to bring many rivers above flood stage.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain caused the Marys River near Philomath to overflow its banks and cause  flooding to surrounding areas. The Marys River crested at 20.1 feet on March 16th at 5 pm PST, 0.1 feet above flood stage.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heavy rainfall caused flash flooding in several areas of Heppner, including residences, the elementary school, sewage treatment plant, city shop, a bank, and the newspaper office. Roadways were also flooded and debris cleanup had to ensue in 10 spots.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: The first in a series of storms brought a long period of very high winds to portions of south central Oregon.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: There were numerous reports of high winds during this interval in zone ORZ031.  The strongest winds occurred near Summer Lake.  The Summer Lake RAWS reported gusts exceeding 57 mph nearly continuously from 17/0938 PST until 19/1438 PST...a 53 hour stretch.  The peak gust was 88 mph at 18/1038 PST.  Occasional gusts meeting warning criteria continued until 19/1838 PST.  The Rock Creek RAWS reported gusts exceeding 57 mph over a much smaller span...18/0627 PST until 18/1127 PST.  The peak gust was 65 mph at 18/1127 PST.  The Summit RAWS reported gusts exceeding 57 mph in several stretches between 18/0503 PST and 19/1203 PST.  The peak gust was 71 mph at 18/1003 PST.  The Coffee Pot RAWS had one gust to 63 mph at 19/1044 PST.  Many spotter reports were also received.  A spotter 9SSE Summer Lake reported a gust to 64 mph at 17/2112 PST, a gust to 72 mph at 18/0740 PST with tree damage and power outages, a gust to 81 mph at 18/0855 PST with blizzard conditions in blowing snow as well as damage to a hay farm and carport, gusts to 65 mph at 19/0000 and a semi-trailer blown off the road at milepost 92 on Highway 31.  A spotter 2NE Valley Falls reported a gust to 69 mph at 18/0558 PST, a gust to 76 mph at 18/1000 PST, and a gust to 77 mph at 19/1246 PST.  A spotter SSW Silver Lake reported a gust to 70 mph at 18/0924 PST.  A member of the public reported a gust to 88 mph at 19/1003 PST with roof damage.  An emergency manager in Summer Lake reported a gust to 71 mph at 19/1128 PST with roof damage at 17 homes in Ana Estates and overturned irrigation pivots.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Another in a series of storms brought high winds to the coast and portions of south central Oregon.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A gust to 59 mph was recorded at the Summer Lake RAWS at 20/1438 PST, then gusts to 60 mph were recorded at 20/1638 and 20/1738 PST.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: With a cold air mass in place over the Pacific Northwest, two strong and very moist Pacific weather systems brought snow to the area with snow levels down to the valley floor. This snow transitioned to rain from south to north across the area, with widespread heavy rains combining with melting snow to bring many rivers above flood stage and a few rivers above major flood stage.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain caused the Luckiamute River at Suver to overflow its banks causing major flooding to surrounding areas. The Luckiamute River crested at 34.2 feet on January 19th at 7 pm PST, 7.2 feet above flood stage.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: The first in a series of storms brought strong winds to the Southern Oregon coast and other isolated areas of Southwest Oregon.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Long Prairie RAWS recorded gusts exceeding 57 mph nearly continuously between 18/0413 and 18/1113 PST.  The peak gust was 66 mph at 18/1113 PST.  The AWOS at the North Bend Municipal Airport recorded gusts to 66 mph at 18/0655 PST, gusts to 59 mph at 18/0635 and 18/0715 PST, and a gust to 58 mph at 18/1055 PST.  An ODOT sensor at Port Orford reported intermittent strong winds over a long period from 18/0202 PST until 18/1330 PST.  A peak gust of 70 mph was recorded at 18/0542 and 18/0548 PST.  A spotter 2SE Coos Bay reported a gust to 67 mph at 18/0924 PST, a spotter 3S Bandon reported a gust to 63 mph at 18/0937 PST.  Reported damage included a partial roof loss in Coos Bay, and downed fences and rain gutters near Bandon.  Gusts as high as 99 mph were recorded at Cape Blanco.  There was also an unconfirmed report of a gust to 113 mph at Charleston.  There were numerous downed trees and power outages.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Very heavy and sustained precipitation led to a widespread flooding event over several counties in Southwest Oregon.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: At 19/2031 PST, a spotter reported that Daniels Creek is flooding onto the roadway.   Some residents cannot leave their homes.  Coos River is also flooding the roadway.  At 19/1730 PST, Another spotter reported that flood waters are 2.5 feet deep in their yard near the confluence of Brummit Creek and the East Fork of the Coquille River.  The water level has been steady for the last 12 hours...it backs up in this area due to constricted flow.  The Millicoma River also flooded during this event, with residents evacuated.  Coos River Highway was shut down from mileposts 13 to 19.   Oregon Highway 38 between Reedsport and Scottsburg was closed due to flooding during this event.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: A trained spotter in Hood River reported 0.25 - 0.50 inch of ice accumulation on top of 4 - 6 inches of snow. The Hood River Valley ODOT reported OR-281 was closed from I-84 to MP 14. A NWS employee in Stevenson reported 0.50 inch ice accumulation. The Hood River County Emergency Manager reported 0.50 inch ice accumulation on top to 7 to 24 inches of snow. He also said there were 100 trees down and power outages. There were nearly 2000 customers without power in Hood River and Mosier according to Pacific Power. Bonneville Power Administration reported the ice taking down a 115 kV main transmission line between Cascade Locks and Hood River.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A major winter storm brought heavy snow to unusually low elevations in southwest Oregon.  The higher elevations also got heavy snow.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A spotter 2N Klamath Falls reported 6.5 inches of snow in 12 hours ending at 29/0700 PST.  A spotter 1NW Klamath Falls reported 6 inches of snow in 10.5 hours ending at 29/0730 PST.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong warm front lifted north across the region accompanied by heavy rain on Sunday. Widespread heavy rain continued to impact the Pacific Northwest Monday and Monday night as the strong and very moist front stalled over the area.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain caused the Wilson River near Tillamook to overflow its banks and flood low lying areas. The Wilson River crested at 14.7 feet, 2.7 feet above flood stage, on November 19th at 4 pm PST.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Very strong winds combined with snowfall created near blizzard conditions in the Oregon Cascades.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Eight to 10 inches of snow fell in the Timberline, Mt Hood and Government Camp areas.  Timberline lodge reported 65 mph sustained winds with gusts to 90 mph at their avalanche sensor near 7000 ft.  Although the wind speeds were a little less in magnitude at lower elevations, the high winds created snow and blowing snow conditions and near blizzard conditions at times.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong low pressure system moved over Western Oregon from the southwest Tuesday night and Wednesday bringing very strong winds to the North and Central Oregon Coast and Coast Range Wednesday morning.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Very strong winds were reported at Cape Foulweather with a gust to 96 kt. Very strong winds were also reported at Yachats with a gust to 95 kt and by a trained spotter in Newport with sustained wind of 52 kt and a gust to 83 kt. Strong gusts were reported at Lincoln City with a gust to 73 kt, at Yaquina Bridge with a gust to 72 kt, at the Dunes RAWS with a gust to 56 kt, at Roads End in Lincoln City with a gust to 51 kt, and gusts to 55 kt were reported at the Newport C-MAN and by a trained spotter in Lincoln City. Sustained wind of 57 kt with gusts to 67 kt were reported at Florence, sustained wind of 43 kt with gusts to 61 kt were reported at the Newport AWOS, sustained wind of 49 kt with gusts to 61 kt were reported at the Newport C-MAN, and sustained wind of 37 kt with gusts to 52 kt were reported at the Newport CWOP. A trained spotter in Florence/Dunes City reported many trees uprooted causing several road closures. Highway 101 south of Dunes City was closed due to downed trees and power lines.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An unusually cold air mass combined with clearing skies to bring freezing temperatures to portions of the southern Oregon coast.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Reported low temperatures ranged from 28 to 32 degrees.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An unusually cold air mass led to late season freezing temperatures over some of the west side valleys of Southwest Oregon.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Reported low temperatures ranged from 27 to 32 degrees.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong Pacific cold front brought strong southerly winds to the North and Central Oregon Coast.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Strong winds were reported at Garibaldi with sustained wind of 50 kt and gusts to 65 kt. Strong winds were also reported at Pacific City with gusts to 56 kt.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong Pacific cold front brought strong southerly winds to the North and Central Oregon Coast.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Strong winds were reported at Yaquina Bridge with sustained wind of 45 kt and gusts to 61 kt. Strong winds were also reported at Yachats with gusts to 59 kt.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: With a cold air mass in place over the Pacific Northwest, two strong and very moist Pacific weather systems brought widespread heavy rains to the Willamette Valley flooding many urban and small streams.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Widespread low-land flooding occurred in Linn County. A car was swept from a Mega Foods parking lot at 7:00 pm PST January 18th, 2011 into the Periwinkle Creek. A family was trapped inside the car. The father and one son were rescued, but the mother and 20-month old child drowned.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong low pressure system brought high winds to portions of Northwest Oregon.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Peak gust of 52 kt measured near Springfield. Greater than 12,000 customers without power in Lane county.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong low pressure system brought high winds to portions of Northwest Oregon.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: More than 2800 customers without power near Independence.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An upper level disturbance moving north on the back side of an upper level ridge combined with a very moist atmosphere to produce local severe thunderstorms and flooding.
An upper level disturbance moving north on the back side of an upper level ridge combined with a very moist atmosphere to produce local severe thunderstorms and flooding.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The strong wind was accompanied by 0.86 inches of rainfall in 50 minutes.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong low pressure system brought high winds to portions of Northwest Oregon.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Greater than 3,100 customers without power in Clackamas county.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Heavy rains brought another round of flooding to the Coquille River.  This flood lasted several days into April.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Coquille River at Coquille exceeded flood stage at 30/1515 PST.  It exceeded moderate flood stage at 31/0045 PST. It crested several times at 24.31 feet between 31/1900 and 31/2045 PST.  It fell below moderate flood state at 03/0700 PST and then fell below flood stage at 04/1300 PST.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A moist southwest flow ahead of an upper level low pressure brought heavy snowfall to the areas just east of the Cascade Mountains and to the Blue Mountains.  Snowfall amounts reported in inches included 2 miles south southwest of Camp Sherman (12), Clear Lake SnoTel (21), 8 miles northeast of Warm Springs (4.5), 9 miles west northwest of Culver (7.5), 1 mile south of Black Butte Ranch (10), High Ridge SnoTel (12), Milk Shakes SnoTel (20), 5 miles north of LaPine (8), 1 mile west of Three Rivers (8), 4 miles west northwest of LaPine (8), Milkshakes SnoTel (18), 1 mile southeast of Flora (8), 10 miles south southeast of Maupin (5.5), and Sunriver (8).

Heavy rains brought another round of flooding to the Coquille River.  This flood lasted several days into April.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The South Fork of the Coquille River at Myrtle Point exceeded flood stage at 31/1600 PST, then exceeded moderate flood stage at 31/1945 PST.  The river crested at 35.79 feet at 30/2330 PST.  The river then fell below moderate flood stage at 31/0745 PST and below flood stage at 01/0730 PST.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong Pacific frontal system brought cold air and low snow levels to central Oregon and the North and Central Oregon Cascades and foothills...resulting in widespread areas of heavy snow.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Yet another front brought yet another round of high winds to the southern Oregon coast.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Flynn Prairie RAWS recorded numerous gusts exceeding 57 mph during this interval.  The peak gust was 67 mph, recorded at 31/0013 and 31/1413 PST.  A spotter 1SE Brookings reported a gust to 60 mph at 31/1144 PST and an estimated gust to 68 mph at 31/1502 PST.  The Coos-Curry Electric Cooperative reported that the storms caused $300,000 damage to the local power grid.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Two incoming cold fronts brought periods of high winds to the southern Oregon coast.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Flynn Prairie RAWS recorded numerous gusts exceeding 57 mph during this interval.  The peak gust was 69 mph, recorded at 29/1313 PST. A spotter SSE Gold Beach reported a southeast gust to 59 mph at 29/1431 PST.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong Pacific cold front brought strong southerly winds to the Oregon Coast and inland to the central Willamette Valley.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Strong winds were reported at Pacific City with sustained wind of 38 kt and gusts to 76 kt. Strong winds were also reported at Clatsop Spit with sustained wind of 53 kt and gusts to 65 kt, at Cape Meares with sustained wind of 42 kt and gusts to 70 kt and at Garibaldi with sustained wind of 50 kt and gusts to 71 kt.  Strong gusts were recorded at Cannon Beach with 55 kt, at Tierra Del Mar with 53 kt, and at  Oceanside with 52 kt.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong Pacific cold front brought strong southerly winds to the Oregon Coast and inland to the central Willamette Valley.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A trained spotter in West Salem reported a wind gust to 59 kt. Other strong wind reports in Salem were sustained wind of 34 kt and gusts to 54 kt, with the Salem ASOS recording a gust to 49 kt. The Statesman Journal in Salem reported numerous power outages around town.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Winds following a strong Pacific cold front increased again, bringing strong northwesterly winds to the North and Central Oregon Coast.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Strong winds were reported at Pacific City with sustained winds of 40 kt and gusts to 58 kt. Strong winds were also reported at Garibaldi with sustained winds of 40 kt and gusts to 51 kt.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong Pacific cold front brought strong southerly winds to the Oregon Coast and inland to the central Willamette Valley.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A trained spotter in Newport recorded a wind gust to 73 kt and reported a metal roof being ripped off the Coast Towing building and  the media in Depoe Bay reported roof damage to a gas station with estimated winds gusting to 60 kt. Strong winds were also reported at Cape Foulweather with sustained wind of 42 kt and gusts to 70 kt and at Yaquina Bridge with sustained wind of 46 kt and gusts to 67 kt. Observers in Newport reported sustained wind ranging from 37 to 40 kt with gusts ranging from 53 to  56 kt. Several reports of strong wind gusts were received from Lincoln City with speeds ranging from 54 to 76 kt and at Yachats with 50 kt.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Winds following a strong Pacific cold front increased again, bringing strong northwesterly winds to the North and Central Oregon Coast.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Strong winds were reported at the Yaquina Bridge with sustained winds of 45 kt and gusts to 63 kt. Strong winds were also reported at Cape Foulweather with sustained winds of 35 kt and gusts to 60 kt and at the Siuslaw Jetty in Florence with sustained winds of 48 kt and gusts to 58 kt. Reports from the Newport area had sustained winds ranging from 37 to 47 kt and gusts ranging from 54 to 60 kt. EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain brought another round of areal flooding to southwest Oregon.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Cold air following a very strong Pacific frontal system brought widespread low elevation snow to Western Oregon Monday night/Tuesday morning. Two more strong weather systems in this series brought heavy rains Wednesday and Thursday. The widespread heavy rain combined with melting snow to bring many rivers above flood stage.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain that fell over the Coast Range combined with snowmelt runoff causing flooding along the Wilson River. The Wilson River at Tillamook crested at 13.5 feet on March 15th at 2 pm PDT, 1.5 feet above flood stage.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A moist air mass combined with surface heating to induce thunderstorms on this day.   Some of the thunderstorms contained strong winds and large hail.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: NWS Doppler radar picked up a 3-body scatter spike and a mesocyclone in this thunderstorm at 23/1538 PST.  The spike was behind a 65-70 dbz core in a severe thunderstorm.  No reports were received, so it is assumed that severe hail fell from this storm.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong Pacific cold front brought strong southerly winds to the north and central Oregon Coast.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Strong wind was reported at Cape Foulweather with a gust to 64 kt. Strong winds were also reported at Yachats with sustained wind of 37 kt and gusts to 53 kt and at Yaquina Bridge with sustained wind of 36 kt.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Increased moisture and prolonged northwest flow assisted in significant upslope snow shower over the Blue Mountains, with Ski Bluewood and Tollgate being the heaviest hit areas. This was a 48 hour warning due to the fact that there was little breaks and there was no real way to separate one storm from another. ||Snow amounts in inches include: Milk Shakes Snotel recorded (8) in the first 12 hours with a 24 hour of (14) and a 48 hour storm total of (18), Tollgate reported (18) in 24 hours, High Ridge Snotel  recorded a 48 storm total of (14), Meacham had a storm total of (8) with occasional blizzard like conditions.  ||Also I-84 was temporarily shut down due to heavy snow and car accidents near Meacham.

CZ_NAME: EAST SLOPES OF THE OREGON CASCADES
EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Lightning caused the Pole Creek wildfire 6 miles southwest of Sisters on September 9.  The fire burned 26,795 acres before being contained October 20. Extensive smoke periodically covered portions of central Oregon causing health concerns.

CZ_NAME: NORTHERN & EASTERN KLAMATH COUNTY & WESTERN LAKE COUNTY
EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Clear skies and long nights allowed freezing temperature to develop in some parts of south central Oregon.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The only reported low temperature in the zone was 26 degrees in Chemult.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: With a cold air mass in place over the Pacific Northwest, two strong and very moist Pacific weather systems brought snow to the area with snow levels down to the valley floor. This snow transitioned to rain from south to north across the area, with widespread heavy rains combining with melting snow to bring many rivers above flood stage and a few rivers above major flood stage.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain combined with snowmelt runoff caused the Pudding River at Aurora to overflow its banks and flood low lying areas. The Pudding River crested at 27.8 feet on January 20th at 12 pm PST, 5.8 feet above flood stage.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong warm front lifted north across the region accompanied by heavy rain on Sunday. Widespread heavy rain continued to impact the Pacific Northwest Monday and Monday night as the strong and very moist front stalled over the area.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain caused the Nehalem River near Vernonia to overflow its banks and flood low lying areas. The Nehalem River crested at 13.4 feet, 1.4 feet above flood stage, on November 19th at 5 pm PST.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Another round of heavy rain impacted the already soaked south Coast...bringing flooding to the area.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Spotters reported up to five inches of rain along with localized flooding with this storm.  Local roads near OBrien were flooded in four different places.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A nearly stationary upper level low pressure area parked near Brookings, Oregon brought abundant moisture across eastern Oregon.  This combined with daytime heating to produce a few severe thunderstorms and locally heavy rainfall.  Lightning caused a few wildfires which were quickly contained.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heavy rainfall caused flood waters and mud to flow into Helix and damage 16 roads, yards, homes, and basements.

This combined with daytime heating to produce a few severe thunderstorms and locally heavy rainfall.  Lightning caused a few wildfires which were quickly contained.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Hail still on the ground 3 hours after the storm passed.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An upper level disturbance moved north across the area during the afternoon and evening. Triple digit temperatures provided ample heating for thunderstorms with gusty damaging winds and locally large hail.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A gustnado within an outflow area destroyed a farm shed, uprooted and broke off several large trees, damaged wheat and an irrigation wheel line.
